  "translation_extracted": "um-ma in-bi-IŠTAR-ma a-na ša ki-ma i-a-tí qí-bi-ma {d}IŠKUR-e-nam ù ki-ma-ḫa-am ÌR-sú a-mì-ša-am e-ta-áb-tù-nim KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na ù 2 ma-na gu5-um-ra-ma a-li wa-áš-bu-ni qá-at-ku-nu li-iṣ-ba-su-nu šu-ma a-ma-a-ma ṣí-lá-{d}IŠKUR lu-kà-ší-su-nu lu a za-al-pá lu a-te-ga-ra-ma lá i-bi-a-at lu-kà-ší-su-nu",
  "translation_english": "Thus, in the presence of Istar, to the one who is like a father to me, the god Enlil, and like a mother to me, the goddess Ishtar, I have given 1 mina and 2 shekels of gold to the sons of Ili-älum, namely, Hanu, son of Itür-ili, and Adad-şulüli, son of Amur-Assur. I have also caused them to be removed, and therefore Assur-kénam-räm, son of Assur-bäni, bears the tablet to you. Before Asqüdum, son of Assur-bäni, before Ikuppij a, son of Assur-dän, and before Li-gépum of the house Qâtum, we entrusted it to them. A copy of the tablet we gave them in their hand. What concerns the matter of the Kärum-house, so [...]en they because of the equalization] words. There corresponding to the silver .... Today they stand for your ...ready. The palace reminds the Kärum, and they are not happy. What concerns Salim-ahum, so we turned to the Kärum two or three times and refuted him before the Kärum. Also, what concerns the matter, because Ennum-Assur, son of Sukkallija, always led us to the Kärum, saying: „I will bring up the share of my brother Ennum-Assur on my responsibility", also because of that matter we refuted him.",
  "llm_opinion": "This page is partially useful for the translation of the tablet, but it contains some unclear or missing parts.",

  "translation_extracted": "There are more occurrences of mutd'ü in a metallurgical context : CCT
5, 34b, ioff.: 6 1/2 GÎN KÙ .BABBAR hi-lcd-at 11 2 ma-na ,sa-ru ßi-im 12 it mu_
td-it KI A , "61/2 shekels of silver, excretion(?) from 2 minas of refined
silver, and loss of weight, due from A" 89) . Lines 26ff. of the same text :
/ GI N mu-su -ka -u 27 s -ti -a i-ba-§i -u 28 1/2 GiN 112/4-0-2L
d -u.
a ma-<sa >-im' ,
" 2/3 shekel of spoiled (silver) is with me ; the loss of weight owing to purifi-
cation (lit.: "washing") amounts to 1/2 shekel." The restoration of the
last word as "washing" is confirmed by several occurrences of this verb,
e.g. ATHE 28, 5ff., where again we meet mutd'ii: 18 ma-na KÙ . BABBAR
ku-nu-ki-kà 6 A ub-lam 6 GÎN 7 i-na ma-sà-im im-ti 8 I GÎN KÙ . BABBAR
a-na na-pd-hi-im 9 ù e-ši ni-di-in 10 3 2/3 GÎN 15 SE mu-si -kà-z 11 ku-nu-ku-
ni i-na É 12 B i-ba-Si 13 16 1/2 GÎN lu ki-ib-sa-tumx 14 lu mu-ta-i, KÙ . BABBAR-
âd-kà 15 i-b[i4-t]i-iq, "i8 minas of silver under your seal(s) A brought me;
it became 6 shekels less by "washing" ; i shekel of silver we gave to the
blacksmith and for wood ; 3% shekels 15 grains of spoiled (silver) are
under our seal(s) in B's house. (In all) your silver has diminished by 16%
shekels, being either kibsdtum or loss of weight" 9°). The "washing" obvi-
ously was a process of refining, performed by a blacksmith, for which he
needed firewood. The result was refined silver, combined with a certain
loss of weight and some inferior silver slag 91) , which nevertheless was
preserved and had some value (it was sealed!) .
The phrase ina masd'im imti is also found in TC 3/1, 36, 44 and 43, 37
both texts describe what happened to an amount of silver sent to Assur,
apparently before it was used to make purchases. That this process of
refining was a factor which could not be neglected in various commercial
undertakings may be learned from CCT 4, 32b, 4ff., a letter found in
beträge" (sub Io) ). Cf. for losses of weight due to melting and refining, LIMET,
Métal, Ch. III, I I off., especially 138ff. . on NE . kû "freinte".
69) The word hïltum with a meaning "(fluid) excretion" is not found in the dic-
tionaries. But cf. CAD H 188b: hïlu, "exudation" and hi/ Jr/ on 189a, d) (cf. AHw
hilum, "Harz", 345b), and perhaps hiltum A (CAD H 188a). Cf. for the basic mean-
ing of the verb hialum, LANDSBERGER, MSL g, 84f. ad I15f.
9°) The reading i-b[i4-t]i-iq, which goes well with the traces, is suggested by the
parallels in BIN 4, 54, 13 and KTP 45,

{
  "found": true,
  "oare_id": "4c55f9b3-62c8-443d-9465-a51384e6811b",
  "source_pdf": "AOATT, 1972.pdf",
  "source_page": "81",
  "language_detected": "en",
  "confidence": 0.82,
  "translation_extracted": "I. SILVER : DEFICIENCIES AS UNDERWEIGHT
51
once expressed in a similar context by means of a punctual N, with in-
gressive meaning, of batâqum : BIN 4, 54, 12f. : URUDU i-na sà-nu-qi-im
8 ma-na 13 i-bi-ti-iq, "when checking the copper 8 minas proved to be
missing" (cf. above note go for two other occurrences of batâqum N, but
this time in a metallurgical context) .
In other texts the permansive batiq, "is missing", is used instead of the
fientic imti f ibbitiq, cf. CAD B 167a. Cf. CCT 2, 2, 6ff. : népis am. .. ' . .. IGI
B aaturma 1/3 mana 2/3 GIN 9 KÙ . BABBAR ba-ti-iq, "I opened the packet in
the presence of B and 202/3 shekels were missing". Cf. also TC 3/1, 36,
18ff., quoted above p. 21f. : isniqûma batiq ; in this case, exceptionally, the
cause of the "underweight" is a weighing mistake, causing an underweight
of 1 mina.
Anyhow this last text proves one used to weigh the silver before send-
ing it off to A§§ur, and hence leaves the possibility open that unsatisfacto-
ry co-ordination of weights on both ends of the commercial line caused
these "deficiencies". ATHE 35, 3of. . hints in this direction. It mentions
that A pays a debt to B in the name of C. After receiving the amount C
protests, stating "your 1-mina-weight is too small", in consequence of
which A pays an additional amount of 11/2 shekels. The text will be dis-
cussed in more detail as no. 2 in Excursus II.
Insufficient co-ordination of weights is not uncommon in ancient Meso-
potamia and generally in the Ancient Near East, as the differences in
weight between well preserved ancient weights show 95) . Also the texts
refer to such differences ; an example may be quoted from ARM 13, 4. In
this text MukanniMum receives 65/6 minas of silver ; but lines 8ff. state :
KÙ . BABBAR §étu ina 5 mana I mana 1/2 mana it 1/3 mana ina NA4. HI . A
NIG . sU bii ja asqulma 2 GIN KÙ . BABBAR imti, "but when I checked (the
weight of ) that silver by means of weights of 5, 1, 1/2
and 1/3 mina, be-
longing to my lord, it was 2 shekels short."",

the presence of B and 202/3 shekels were missing". Cf. also TC

pron.suff. with the inf.Gt of labäsum 159). Though we meet in these texts

infer from the fact that these textiles here occur together with labäsum,
that they were all garments.

  "confidence": 0.82,
  "translation_extracted": "menuniânum - ébipisum 171
Besides CCT 5, 12a, 9-where this textile is shown to belong to the cate-
gory of sab/pd/tinnû we meet it in : LB 1268, 15 (cf. above p. 38), pre-
ceded by burâ'um and nibrârum and followed by pirikannum; in BIN 4,
51, 39 : 7 TUG DI-zA-BU-i , along with "woollen fleeces" ; in KTB 7, 5f. in
an enumeration of import-textiles and tin : 20 LA 2 TI-ZA-BU ; in ICK 2,
160, 3ff., mentioning a payment for a slave and a Df-ZA-BU-um ; and in
BIN 6, 84, 6 (cf. 14) : one TiJG Df-ZA-BA-am has been sent to a woman (in
Aur) . I cannot make any suggestion about its meaning, save noting
some relation with rough woollen textile products.
17. menuniı num
Apparently a native textile product from Anatolia. In CCT 4, 27a, 'if.
mentioned together with wool, woollen fleeces and pirikanniı. BIN 4, 78,
6ff. contains an order from Inbistar to buy : 6 pi-ri-kà-ni ? sa lu-bu-us şiı-
ha-ri 8 lu me-nu-ni-a-nu lu i-bi/pi-§u 9 lu pi!-ri-kà-ni sa a-na 1° lu-bu-§i-im
da-nu-ni-ma 11 pé-lu-tdm ii-bu-lu-ni, " pirikannû being/for garments for
servants, or m., or éb/yisû, or pirikannû which are strong enough to be
used as clothing and which have a reddish colour(? ?)". KTH 1, 17f.
mentions m. beside kusitum-garments from Mamma and woollen fleeces;
KTH 6, 6 shows they can be used as implied by BIN 4, 78 for clothing
of the personnel, and cost only 1 shekel of silver apiece. EL 131, 26 with
the unique writing 1 TvG me-nu-né-nu-um--lists one m. among goods
shipped to Assur.
No etymology is known; BILGIÇ App. 54 regards m. as a native Ana-
tolian appellative, which is possible.
i8. éb/pïsum
Occurrences : in BIN 4, 78, 6-just quoted as a product comparable to,
or a variety of, the pirikannum. Similarly VAT 9290, 6-7 (quoted p. 126)
pirikanni a-BI-if 7 ta-pd-tim, for which line 21 simply writes pirikanni,
showing the last words to be a description or specification of pirikanniı :
"e. of wool". TC 3/2, 132, I mentions makühu and e-BI-tu; AnOr VI, 15,
3f. describes a transport unit as consisting of 1 donkey, 27 kutânü-textiles
and one [e]-BI-sa-am (cf. for the first vowel line 19) ; KTK 11, 16' mentions
a loss of 20 T[TG e-BI-sf'.
Word and meaning : normally it is written with e- as first vowel, but
once with i- (BIN 4, 78, 6). LEWY 2958, 9865 takes the word to be episum
adducing also TC 3/2, 91, 33f.: sa 7 GIN KÛ . BABBAR e-Bl-§u-um, "an e.,
worth 7 shekels of silver" and to mean "blanket", used as a saddle rug",
  "
